globalEvents.title: Jamie Dimon says he doesn't know if Iran war will be "tipping point" for recession
globalEvents.detail: Jamie Dimon told "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil that "what's more important for the future of the world is that this war successfully conclude."

globalEvents.title: Gunmen kill at least 20 in Nigeria Palm Sunday attack, curfew imposed
globalEvents.detail: Nigeria gunmen killed at least 20 people in a Palm Sunday attack in Plateau state's Jos North area, according to residents, prompting a 48-hour curfew.

globalEvents.detail: Neighbours tried to fend the dog off 84-year-old John McColl who was left seriously injured in the attack

globalEvents.title: Senior Canadian commander charged by military police in compensation probe
globalEvents.detail: Canada's military police charged Col. James Smith with prejudice to good order regarding compensation irregularities while he commanded Task Force Latvia. Legal proceedings are pending.

globalEvents.title: Gulf war anxiety hits Kerala homes, clouds election mood
globalEvents.detail: Keralites with loved ones in West Asia are deeply worried about the current turmoil. Fears about their family's safety and job stability for expatriates have intensified. The state's economy, which leans heavily on remittances, could experience significant challenges. Many residents are fervently wishing for a quick end to the hostilities.

globalEvents.title: Rising fuel costs put brakes on CV financing momentum
globalEvents.detail: Rising fuel costs due to the Iran war are pressuring bank loans for commercial vehicles in India. Fleet operators face higher expenses, leading them to delay new purchases. This slowdown affects credit demand for commercial vehicles. Small operators are particularly vulnerable, risking increased loan defaults.

globalEvents.title: How Iran war is disrupting fertiliser supply, impacting India's agricultural sector
globalEvents.detail: Field reports from Madhya Pradesh show farmers grappling with input shortages and rising costs, with farmers, traders, and experts warning the crisis may deepen if supply chains are not stabilised soon.

globalEvents.title: Petrol vapour ignition sparks fire at Shakur Basti railway station, no casualties
globalEvents.detail: Fire at Shakur Basti railway station after petrol vapour ignition from tank wagon leak; blaze quickly contained, no injuries, inquiry and technical inspection underway.

globalEvents.title: Fire in petrol wagon at Shakur Basti railway yard, no injuries
globalEvents.detail: A fire in a petrol tank wagon at Delhi's Shakur Basti yard was quickly controlled with no injuries. An inquiry is underway to determine the cause. | Latest News Delhi

globalEvents.title: Fire at SE Delhi residential building, 10 rescued
globalEvents.detail: Ten people, including a child, were rescued from a fire in a Delhi building. The blaze was controlled within 20 minutes, with no injuries reported. | Latest News Delhi

globalEvents.title: Security audit by police finds glaring gaps at most city temples
globalEvents.detail: Delhi Police security audit finds major gaps at Delhi temples; recommends metal detectors, baggage scanners, CCTV monitoring, PA systems, fire safety and evacuation plans.

globalEvents.title: Iran Ready To End War? Stock Market, Dow Jones, And Nasdaq Jump On President Pezeshkian's Statement
globalEvents.detail: Iran’s president, Masoud Pezeshkian, says the country is ready to end the war if strict conditions, including guarantees against future attacks, are met. The stock market, Dow Jones, and Nasdaq rallied after Pezeshkian's statement., Middle East, Times Now

globalEvents.title: Daily U.S. Stock Market Outlook: Stocks Explode As The U.S.-Iran War May Come To An End
globalEvents.detail: US stock benchmarks are now attempting a more significant bounce from recent lows as the war narrative eases.

globalEvents.title: The soaring stock market is sending a message: It expects the Iran war to end soon
globalEvents.detail: All three major US indexes spiked more than 2% on Tuesday.

globalEvents.title: British Broadcast Engineer Found Dead In Mumbai Hotel Under Suspicious Circumstances During IPL Coverage
globalEvents.detail: A 76-year-old British broadcast engineer working for IPL coverage was found dead in a Mumbai hotel room on March 30. He was discovered unconscious by staff and declared dead at a hospital. Police have registered an ADR and said no foul play is suspected so far, with the post-mortem completed and investigation ongoing.

globalEvents.title: US Customs Agency Says Tariff Refund System Progressing, but Payments May Take up to 45 Days

globalEvents.title: Mike Evans decreases 49ers' WR drama vs Brandon Aiyuk. Will he make plays?
globalEvents.detail: The 49ers' wide receiver room has gotten serious. The health of Mike Evans and Christian Kirk will determine if that's a good thing.

globalEvents.title: Voluntary assisted dying: Why it matters for MND sufferer Jason Smith
globalEvents.detail: Jason Smith, diagnosed with motor neurone disease before 40, is registered with the ACT's VAD scheme. His wife Anthea wants to reduce barriers to access.

globalEvents.title: Koye Pharma next on Warburg's health play
globalEvents.detail: Global private equity fund Warburg Pincus is nearing a deal to buy Koye Pharma. The Mumbai-based drugmaker focuses on women's health, respiratory care, and anti-diabetes drugs. This acquisition, valued at approximately ₹300 crore, marks Warburg Pincus's third investment in India's growing medicine market. Koye Pharma has shown strong growth recently, with sales around ₹160 crore.

globalEvents.title: Planetary health diet meets key nutrient needs during early pregnancy
globalEvents.detail: Following The Planetary Health Diet in pregnancy can meet key nutrient requirements, suggesting that women who eat more sustainably in pregnancy may have higher intakes of several key-pregnancy-related nutrients, including folate, iron, calcium, and dietary fiber.

globalEvents.title: Ottawa taking over compensation to address backlog
globalEvents.detail: The federal public health agency is taking over administration of a program that compensates people who have been injured by vaccines, months after Health Minister Marjorie Michel ordered an audit of the program following complaints about its administration.

globalEvents.title: 'There is a rule': West Bengal chief election officer dismisses TMC's 'voter list manipulation' allegations
globalEvents.detail: India News: Amid a heated political row in West Bengal over alleged voter list manipulation allegation levied by Trinamool Congress (TMC) ahead of the Assembly el.

globalEvents.title: Court permanently blocks Trump’s order to cut NPR and PBS funding
globalEvents.detail: “It is difficult to conceive of clearer evidence that a government action is targeted at viewpoints that the President does not like and seeks to squelch."

globalEvents.title: Congress to protest against FCRA amendment Bill
globalEvents.detail: Congress plans a protest against FCRA amendments, arguing they threaten NGOs and minority-run community organizations.

globalEvents.title: Bihu beats add festive twist to hectic poll roster

globalEvents.title: Ford’s BlueCruise System Faces NTSB Scrutiny After Fatal Crashes
globalEvents.detail: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) criticized Ford's BlueCruise driver assistance system after two fatal crashes. The lack of government regulations and claims made by automakers were questioned. Investigations highlighted issues such as system limitations, driver complacency, and a need for comprehensive safety guidelines. Ford has yet to comment.

adminEvents.title: WAR/POLITICS - 2026 Mar
adminEvents.detail: Ukraine war escalation continues across Eastern Europe as multiple drone incidents were recorded across Baltic airspace in late March 2026, with at least 3 confirmed airspace violations reported by Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia within a 48-hour window, intensifying NATO air-defense readiness along the 1,450 km eastern flank.

adminEvents.title: TECHNOLOGY - 2026 Mar
adminEvents.detail: Agentic AI transition accelerates in 2026, with over 60% of new enterprise AI deployments shifting from generative models to autonomous task-executing systems, while more than 12 major countries expand “digital sovereignty” policies aimed at controlling AI infrastructure, including GPU allocation exceeding 10,000 H100-equivalent units in national compute programs.

adminEvents.title: ECONOMIC - 2026/Mar
adminEvents.detail: Global economic fragmentation deepens as analysts describe a persistent K-shaped recovery, with household debt linked to buy-now-pay-later services exceeding double-digit annual growth rates in multiple OECD economies, while supply-chain volatility affects more than 30% of critical manufacturing inputs across semiconductor and energy sectors.
